items.title:
[负面] THIS IS NOT A "GOOD GAME"!

Its a Gacha game with ...
items.summary:
THIS IS NOT A "GOOD GAME"!

Its a Gacha game with basically a turn based card battler attached to it.


PROS: 
- If you love Gacha, you  will love this because the Gacha is really rewarding. You get alot of new and cool stuff as a free2play player and getting and spending all the currencies makes for a nice dopamine spike.  
- You can unlock (almost?) everything without spending money even though it will take a year or two of daily grinding.
- Artwork and music are solid.

CONS:
- where do i start? About 50% of the ingame time is spent clicking through menues, collecting currencies and spending currencies. Its really boring and a ton of busywork.
- the other 50% is split 50/50 between dialogue and gameplay/actual combat. The combat is SOLID but not great. You have a continuously growing pool of now 60+ characters that are split into 4 factions. You can combine 4 characters out of 2 different factions. So any given team currently has a pool of about 30 characters. Each character brings a unique ability, a couple of passives and three unique cards to the game.

Sounds good at first but since you have to collect and upgrade every character for a long long time, as a f2p player you will use the same team for many months. 
Since you cant modify your deck in any way and every "run" is a short series of single digit combats with a boss in the end, the game is suuuuuuuuper repetitive. 

- and how is the combat itself? Sadly not very good. Every possible team combo has a one dimensional gameplay loop that often depends slightly on drawluck and heavily on having upgraded your stuff enough via metacurrencies and time spent.

If you would remove every gacha feature and only have the core gameplay, this would be rated very lowly. People just hype it because its free2play and they love gacha. 

Conclusion: unless you love gacha and are addicted to the dopamine rush, stay the hell away from this game.
